serving.natural_language_portion: One packet drink of Marigold Chrysanthemum Tea is typically 250g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Packaged Commercial Beverage Nutritional Reference
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from similar sweetened chrysanthemum tea products in USDA FoodData Central, as exact Marigold brand data was not available. Confidence in macros is medium due to direct comparison.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database - Sweetened Tea
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated from sweetened tea beverages with similar sugar content (10g/100g) from the University of Sydney GI Database. The specific GI for Marigold Chrysanthemum Tea (Packet) is not available.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (10g/100g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB).
hypertension.guidance: Avoid sugar-sweetened beverages due to their independent association with increased blood pressure risk, regardless of sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (10g/100g) and zero fiber in a liquid form.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Strictly avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage as liquid sugars cause rapid and significant postprandial glucose spikes, worsening glycemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High intake of added sugars, particularly from sugar-sweetened beverages.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Limit or eliminate sugar-sweetened beverages to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, as high sugar intake contributes to dyslipidemia and inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Marigold Chrysanthemum Tea (Packet) is a popular pre-packaged, ready-to-drink beverage, typically served chilled. It is a sweetened tea made from water, sugar, and chrysanthemum extract. The tea has a floral aroma and a sweet, refreshing taste. Nutritionally, it is high in sugar (10g/100g) with negligible protein, fat, and dietary fiber, making it an ultra-processed beverage. Its high sugar content and liquid form contribute to a medium glycemic index, posing a high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Cardiovascular Disease due to rapid glucose absorption and independent links to chronic disease risks. It is considered halal, vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free.
common_user_queries: Nutritional value of packet chrysanthemum tea
Is chrysanthemum tea good for diabetes?

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is adapted from similar sweetened chrysanthemum tea products in USDA FoodData Central. GI is an analogous estimate. Local names and aliases are based on common knowledge and translation for Singaporean context.
notes: Protein_g, fat_g, saturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, dietary_fibre_g, and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero by product formulation and typical composition of sweetened tea beverages. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1 as detailed micronutrient data for this specific type of packaged beverage is not readily available in approved sources.
